description: Finalize the Miss Lana documentation canon after the first brand/domain/contact synchronization. Remove or neutralize superseded duplicate canon files, resolve the remaining cross-document contradictions, standardize project-owned public copy to the locked Theatre spelling, lock /school-shows as the canonical B2B route, make verified reviews/testimonials an explicit launch trust requirement without inventing any reviews, update current-vs-legacy site status, standardize the Phosphor React package, and clarify form-confirmation behavior. This is a documentation canon-finalization task only.

primary_authorities: Current operator decision in project chat dated 2026-06-25
current_truth: The locked public brand is 'Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre'.
The primary production domain is misslanatheatre.com and the current production website is already live on that domain.
The primary public email is info@misslanatheatre.com.
The primary public phone is (323) 903-2039; the second phone is reserve-only.
SMS, email, and WhatsApp are accepted; written contact is preferred.
Svitlana currently answers inquiries herself within 1-2 business days.
The customer must receive a clear on-screen form confirmation. A full copy of the submitted form must not be emailed by default; a short confirmation email may be added later if operationally useful.
The approved on-screen confirmation copy is: 'Thank you! We've received your request. Miss Lana will reply by text, email, or WhatsApp within 1-2 business days.'

Project-owned public brand and marketing copy should use Theatre consistently. American 'theater' forms remain valid only in SEO query examples, third-party proper names, legacy URLs, quotations, historical references, schema vocabulary, and technical identifiers.
The canonical B2B route is /school-shows. /for-preschools is obsolete as a canonical route and may only remain as a redirect reference if it has ever been published.

Verified testimonials/reviews are in launch scope as a trust requirement. Only real reviews with permission may be published. No review content may be invented. Initial launch target: at least five strong verified reviews, ideally from both parents and preschool/school representatives.
The current site is misslanatheatre.com and remains in content, conversion, SEO, and trust-layer refinement. magic-castle-puppet-theater.com is a legacy domain only.
misslanatheater.com is a protective alternate domain only and should redirect to misslanatheatre.com.
The canonical React icon package is @phosphor-icons/react. The deprecated phosphor-react package must not be prescribed for new implementation.
Trademark clearance remains required before final logo, print, merchandise, or permanent branded production assets.
Travel surcharges, exact cancellation rules, insurance/background-check claims, and unresolved show titles remain open owner questions and must not be invented.
precedence_rules: This task's current_truth overrides older contradictory statements in project documentation.
BRAND.md is authoritative for brand, domain, email naming, and service-line naming.
PROJECT_BRIEF.md is authoritative for business facts and current-site status.
SITE_STRUCTURE_AND_BLOCKS.md is authoritative for detailed page routes and block architecture; /school-shows is canonical.
DESIGN_SYSTEM.md is authoritative for implementation dependencies and design tokens; @phosphor-icons/react is canonical.
Do not modify third-party proper names, official venue names, legacy URLs, SEO query examples, schema type names, or quoted source language merely to force Theatre spelling.

If multiple files with the same logical role exist, retain exactly one canonical active version in the documented canonical path and remove/archive superseded repository duplicates according to repository policy.
forbidden_change_classes: Production website code or UI changes
DNS, redirects, email, hosting, deployment, GBP, Search Console, analytics, or schema implementation
Invented reviews, testimonials, client names, ratings, or permissions
Invented travel fees, business hours, cancellation rules, insurance, background checks, or school credentials
Final trademark, logo, or print decisions
Deletion of legitimate third-party, historical, SEO, legacy, or technical references

required_files: tung_system_specification.md or an approved repository v2-full template/runtime
prerequisites.blocking_condition: If no canonical v2-full specification/template/runtime is available, do not invent the schema. Report the governance blocker. If an approved runtime already validated MISS_LANA_CANON_SYNC_001, this task may use the same approved v2-full execution contract.

in_scope: Inventory all active and superseded copies of the eleven project canon/operational documents in the repository.
Ensure exactly one active canonical copy exists for each logical document and that indexes/instructions reference the canonical path.
Remove, archive, or mark superseded duplicate repository copies according to repository policy; do not silently delete historical source material where archival is required.
Update all project-owned public brand and marketing copy to Theatre spelling, including taglines, hero examples, CTA copy, service descriptions, and reasons-to-believe.
Preserve American theater SEO keywords, third-party names, legacy URLs, schema TheaterEvent, quotations, historical notes, and technical identifiers where correct.
Correct all documentation to identify https://misslanatheatre.com as the current production website and magic-castle-puppet-theater.com as legacy only.
Replace obsolete language saying the current site still needs to be rebranded or moved; describe it as live and under content/conversion/SEO refinement.
Lock /school-shows as the canonical B2B route in all IA, sitemap, navigation, SEO, and build documents.
Remove /for-preschools as a canonical destination; retain only as a documented redirect source if repository history or production evidence shows it was published.
Change reviews/testimonials from excluded/later to a launch trust requirement with [OWNER/CONTENT] status and an initial target of at least five verified reviews.
Explicitly prohibit invented reviews and require permission/source attribution appropriate to the review source.
Normalize all new implementation guidance to @phosphor-icons/react and mark phosphor-react as deprecated/historical where it appears in research history.
Clarify form behavior: on-screen confirmation required; no full submitted-form copy by default; short confirmation email optional later.
Create final residue, contradiction, duplicate, and route scans and a reviewer-grade closure report.
out_of_scope: Actually creating redirects, changing routes, configuring DNS, or migrating domains.
Collecting or writing review content; this task only makes reviews a documented requirement.
Resolving travel fees, exact business hours, cancellation/deposit rules, insurance, background checks, show-title decisions, or public-school procurement.
Changing third-party proper names or SEO evidence.
Changing design tokens, page visual design, or product pricing.
Deleting duplicate files outside the repository/project workspace or manipulating Claude/ChatGPT Project knowledge UI; report such external duplicates for manual removal.

non_goals: Do not perform a blind Theater-to-Theatre replacement.
Do not invent, paraphrase, or fabricate reviews.
Do not implement website, route, redirect, form, email, GBP, or SEO runtime changes.
Do not resolve unrelated owner questions.
Do not rewrite the entire marketing strategy.
Do not remove valid theater spellings in SEO queries, third-party names, legacy URLs, schema, or quotations.
Do not claim external Project knowledge duplicates were removed unless the executor actually has access to that UI/system.
Do not treat reviews as already collected merely because they are now in launch scope.
forbidden_execution_patterns: No global replacement without per-occurrence semantic classification.
No deleting all older files solely by filename; inspect repository path, references, and history first.
No invented testimonial text, names, star ratings, school names, or permissions.
No converting /for-preschools into /school-shows in external links without documenting redirect implications.
No claiming a route redirect or domain redirect exists unless separately verified.
No mixing project-owned public copy with SEO keyword spelling rules.
No prescribing both phosphor-react and @phosphor-icons/react as interchangeable current dependencies.
No leaving contradictory current-site descriptions in lower sections after updating document headers.
No completion claim without duplicate scan, contradiction scan, route scan, dependency scan, and full diff review.
execution_guardrails: Create a pre-edit matrix listing every logical document, all repository paths found, active/superseded status, and intended action.
Classify every remaining 'theater' occurrence as PROJECT_PUBLIC_COPY, SEO_KEYWORD, THIRD_PARTY_NAME, LEGACY_URL, SCHEMA_OR_TECHNICAL, QUOTATION, HISTORICAL_NOTE, or ERROR.
Use Theatre for PROJECT_PUBLIC_COPY and project-owned brand/service names.
Preserve all other semantically valid categories exactly unless a separate factual correction is required.
Search for old current-site wording such as current site, needs rebrand, domain available/free, only call us, no gallery, no online form, or old URL as current; verify context manually.
Set reviews to [OWNER/CONTENT] or equivalent launch-required status, not READY and not completed.
State launch review target as a target/recommendation, not an existing asset count.
Standardize canonical route examples to /school-shows and document /for-preschools only as a possible redirect source.
Standardize implementation dependency to @phosphor-icons/react; in the research document, preserve historical mention only if clearly marked superseded.
Keep the approved form success copy unchanged except for typographic punctuation.
Retain the exact primary phone, email, and domain values.
Review headings, tables, code fences, diagrams, URL trees, examples, changelogs, and cross-links—not only prose.
Use git diff --check and full diff review before closure.
If duplicate files exist only in an external project-knowledge UI, create a manual cleanup list rather than claiming repository deletion.

steps.title: Inventory canonical paths and duplicate versions
actions: Locate every repository copy of the eleven project documents by filename and logical role.
Record path, version/date header, brand/domain state, inbound references, and active/superseded recommendation.
Create docs/reports/MISS_LANA_CANON_FINALIZATION_002_pre_edit_matrix.md.
If duplicates are external to the repository, list them separately as MANUAL PROJECT-KNOWLEDGE CLEANUP.
steps.expected_outcome: There is an evidence-based duplicate/canonical-path plan before editing or removal.

steps.title: Clean duplicate canon and repair references
actions: Retain exactly one active canonical repository copy per logical document.
Remove or archive superseded duplicates according to repository policy.
Update README, project instructions, indexes, and cross-links to canonical paths.
Do not claim external knowledge duplicates were removed; produce a manual removal list when applicable.
steps.expected_outcome: Future agents resolve one canonical file per document and no repository reference points to a superseded copy.
affected_files: Canonical indexes/instructions and superseded duplicate files as discovered

steps.title: Finalize brand spelling in public copy
actions: Change project-owned taglines, hero examples, reasons-to-believe, service copy, and CTA examples from theater to theatre.
Keep the locked brand name and service-line spelling consistent.
Preserve SEO keywords, third-party names, legacy URLs, TheaterEvent schema, quotations, and historical notes.
Add a brief style rule distinguishing public copy from SEO/technical exceptions.
steps.expected_outcome: The project presents one public spelling without corrupting evidence or technical vocabulary.

steps.title: Correct current-site and legacy-site status
actions: Identify misslanatheatre.com as the current production site in all relevant documents.
Describe the current site as live and under content, conversion, trust, and SEO refinement.
Identify magic-castle-puppet-theater.com as legacy only.
Remove obsolete statements implying the brand/domain migration has not happened.
Keep redirect, GBP, Search Console, and listing work as unverified implementation tasks unless evidence proves completion.
steps.expected_outcome: No document confuses the live new site with the legacy site or an unstarted redesign.

steps.title: Lock the canonical B2B route
actions: Use /school-shows as the only canonical B2B destination in sitemap, navigation, page blueprints, metadata examples, and build order.
Remove /for-preschools as a canonical route.
If /for-preschools was previously published or is referenced externally, document a future 301 redirect requirement without implementing it.
steps.expected_outcome: All documentation agrees on /school-shows and no competing canonical B2B route remains.

steps.title: Move verified reviews into launch scope
actions: Replace statements that reviews are not needed or are out of scope.
Define testimonials/reviews as a launch trust requirement with [OWNER/CONTENT] status.
Set an initial target of at least five real verified reviews, ideally from both parents and preschool/school representatives.
Require permission/source verification and prohibit invented review copy.
Do not mark the requirement complete or imply existing review inventory.
steps.expected_outcome: Every relevant document treats reviews consistently as required but not yet collected/verified.

steps.title: Normalize icon dependency and form-confirmation rules
actions: Set @phosphor-icons/react as the only current implementation package.
Mark phosphor-react references in research history as superseded or replace them where they are prescriptive.
Clarify that on-screen confirmation is required, the full submitted-form copy is not emailed by default, and a short confirmation email may be added later.
Preserve the approved on-screen confirmation text.
steps.expected_outcome: No dependency conflict or form-confirmation ambiguity remains.

steps.title: Run final contradiction and residue audits
actions: Scan for obsolete brand/domain/email references.
Scan for project-owned public-copy theater occurrences and classify every remainder.
Scan for reviews excluded/later/not needed wording.
Scan for /for-preschools canonical references.
Scan for phosphor-react prescriptive references.
Scan for old URL described as current and new URL described as proposed/free.
Scan for duplicate logical documents and broken cross-links.
steps.expected_outcome: No unexplained contradiction or residue remains.

steps.title: Create reviewer-grade finalization report
actions: Review the complete diff file by file.
Create docs/reports/MISS_LANA_CANON_FINALIZATION_002_report.md.
Create docs/reviews/MISS_LANA_CANON_FINALIZATION_002_review.md.
Include duplicate actions, preserved theater exceptions, route decision, review status, dependency decision, current-site status, unresolved owner questions, and verification results.
Declare READY FOR SITE COPY SYNC only if every success criterion passes.
steps.expected_outcome: The finalized canon is documented, reviewable, and safe for the next site-copy task.

success_criteria: Exactly one active canonical repository copy exists for each logical project document, or external duplicates are explicitly listed for manual cleanup.
All project-owned public brand and marketing copy uses Theatre spelling.
Every remaining theater occurrence is valid and classified as SEO, third-party, legacy, schema/technical, quotation, or historical context.
misslanatheatre.com is consistently described as the current live production site.
magic-castle-puppet-theater.com is consistently described as legacy only.
No document says the new domain is merely free, proposed, awaiting purchase, or awaiting migration.
/school-shows is the sole canonical B2B route in all active documents.
/for-preschools appears only as a potential redirect-source note if historically needed.
Verified testimonials/reviews are consistently in launch scope with [OWNER/CONTENT] status and no invented content.
The launch target of at least five verified reviews is presented as a target, not an existing fact.
@phosphor-icons/react is the only current prescribed React icon package.
The form rules require on-screen confirmation, do not send a full form copy by default, and allow a future short confirmation email.
The primary domain, email, phone, written-contact methods, responder, and response window remain correct.
No unrelated code, pricing, legal, design-token, or business-fact changes were introduced.
Final report and reviewer-grade review exist and are readable.

required_commands: rg -n --hidden --glob '*.md' "Live theater|children['’]s theater|theater troupe|theater show|values-driven theater|30\+ years of.*theater" .
manual_checks: Confirm every active logical document has one canonical repository path.
Confirm external knowledge duplicates, if any, are listed for manual removal and not falsely claimed deleted.
Confirm every remaining project-owned public phrase uses Theatre.
Confirm valid American SEO phrases and third-party names were preserved.
Confirm misslanatheatre.com is the current site and the old domain is legacy only.
Confirm reviews are required but not represented as already collected.
Confirm no review text, names, ratings, or permissions were invented.
Confirm @phosphor-icons/react is the only current prescribed dependency.
Confirm the form confirmation distinction is clear and consistent.
Confirm all Markdown tables, trees, links, code fences, and changelogs remain readable.
Confirm no website implementation was claimed or performed.
Confirm non_goals, forbidden_execution_patterns, and execution_guardrails were respected.
failure_conditions: A superseded duplicate remains active or referenced as canonical without explanation.
Any unexplained project-owned Theater public-copy occurrence remains.
Any valid SEO, third-party, legacy, schema, quotation, or historical spelling is incorrectly changed.
Any active document still describes the legacy domain as the current site.
Any active document still treats /for-preschools as canonical.
Any active document still says reviews are not needed or excluded from launch.
phosphor-react remains prescribed as a current implementation package.
Any required report/review artifact is missing.
Production code or external infrastructure is modified.

modified_files: Canonical indexes/README files if they reference superseded paths
conditional_actions: Archive/remove superseded repository duplicates according to repository policy.
Create a MANUAL PROJECT-KNOWLEDGE CLEANUP list if duplicate files exist only outside the repository.
report_requirements: List the one canonical path for every logical document.
List every duplicate and its disposition: retained, archived, removed, or manual external cleanup.
Summarize Theatre public-copy changes and preserved theater exceptions.
Confirm /school-shows as canonical and document any future redirect requirement.
Confirm reviews are launch-required but not yet collected/verified.
Confirm current and legacy site status.
List unresolved owner questions without solving them.
Include command pass/fail results and final READY/BLOCKED status.
final_response_requirements: State completed, partially completed, or blocked.
List modified, created, archived, and removed files.
List any external duplicate files requiring manual deletion.
Summarize preserved exceptions and unresolved questions.
Do not claim website code, redirects, routes, email delivery, reviews, or infrastructure were implemented.

closure_sequence: Step 1 — Scope verification: confirm only documentation, duplicate-canon cleanup, indexes, and required reports were changed.
Step 2 — Duplicate verification: confirm one active canonical path per logical document and document external duplicates separately.
Step 3 — Public-copy verification: confirm project-owned copy uses Theatre and all preserved theater exceptions are semantically valid.
Step 4 — Status verification: confirm current-site, legacy-site, domain, email, phone, and contact workflow are consistent.
Step 5 — Route/review/dependency verification: confirm /school-shows, launch-required verified reviews, and @phosphor-icons/react are consistently locked.
Step 6 — Reviewer-grade diff review: inspect all edits, removals, archives, links, tables, trees, fences, and changelogs; save review artifact.
Step 7 — Command verification: run every required command and resolve all in-scope failures.
Step 8 — Deliverable verification: confirm all reports and review artifacts exist and are readable.
Step 9 — Execution-safety confirmation: explicitly confirm no non_goals were fixed, no forbidden patterns were violated, and all guardrails were respected.
Step 10 — Closure statement: declare READY FOR SITE COPY SYNC only when every success criterion passes; otherwise declare BLOCKED with exact reasons.

post_actions: Do not edit production website code as part of this task.
After successful closure, use only the finalized canonical files for the site-wide public copy inventory and synchronization task.
Create a separate TUNG v2-full for website text/code synchronization, beginning with header, footer, contacts, booking form, and Home page.
Create separate implementation tasks for redirects/routes, form delivery, reviews collection workflow, GBP, Search Console, schema, and analytics.
